articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Context
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a context window exceeding 1 million tokens. The model demonstrates significant gains in long-horizon planning, mathematical proof generation, and agentic task completion. Independent benchmarks show a 35% improvement over GPT-4.5 on complex multi-step coding and scientific reasoning tasks.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Models Enable Zero-Shot Dexterous Manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind published results from its Gemini Robotics program showing foundation models that allow humanoid and multi-fingered robotic hands to perform complex dexterous manipulation tasks with zero-shot transfer from simulation. The system uses a vision-language-action architecture that generalizes across unseen objects and environments, marking a significant step toward general-purpose robotic manipulation.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Tool-Use Framework for Autonomous Enterprise Workflows
articles.summary: Anthropic released a production-grade tool-use and orchestration framework for Claude, enabling autonomous multi-step enterprise workflows with built-in sandboxing, audit logging, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints. The framework supports persistent agent sessions that can interact with databases, APIs, and file systems while maintaining Constitutional AI safety constraints. Early enterprise adopters report 40-60% reductions in back-office process cycle times.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Hyundai Unveil Atlas Commercial Deployment for Automotive Assembly
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics announced the first large-scale commercial deployment of its electric Atlas humanoid robot in Hyundai automotive assembly plants in South Korea. The robots handle heavy part transportation, precision component insertion, and quality inspection tasks alongside human workers. The deployment represents 75 units operating across three facilities with plans to scale to 500 by year-end.

articles.title: Meta Releases Open-Source Llama 4 Behemoth with Mixture-of-Experts at 2 Trillion Parameters
articles.summary: Meta publicly released the full weights of Llama 4 Behemoth, a 2-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model that rivals proprietary frontier models on reasoning, code generation, and multilingual tasks. The model uses a novel routing architecture that activates only 200B parameters per inference pass, making it runnable on high-end multi-GPU setups. The open-source release has reignited debate around responsible release practices for frontier-class models.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm Gate-All-Around Process Node
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed it has entered volume production on its A14 (1.4nm-class) process node using gate-all-around nanosheet transistors at its Fab 20 in Tainan. The node delivers an 18% speed improvement and 25% power reduction over N2, targeting next-generation AI accelerator and mobile SoC designs. Apple, NVIDIA, and AMD are confirmed as first customers with products expected in late 2026.

articles.title: Figure AI Raises $1.5B Series C as Humanoid Robots Enter Warehouse Logistics at Scale
articles.summary: Figure AI closed a $1.5 billion Series C round at an $18 billion valuation, fueled by commercial contracts with Amazon and BMW for warehouse logistics and light manufacturing. The company's Figure 03 robot demonstrates improved autonomous navigation, package handling, and collaborative task execution using onboard vision-language models. The funding will accelerate production to thousands of units per year at its new South Carolina facility.

articles.title: Microsoft and Pacific Northwest National Lab Synthesize New Solid-State Electrolyte Discovered by AI
articles.summary: Microsoft and PNNL announced successful lab synthesis and testing of a novel lithium-ion solid-state electrolyte material first identified by Microsoft's AI-driven materials discovery platform in 2024. The material demonstrates ionic conductivity on par with liquid electrolytes while maintaining structural stability through 1,000+ charge cycles. This validates the AI-to-lab materials pipeline and could accelerate the development of safer, higher-density batteries for EVs and grid storage.
